Campus development plan review in accordance with Article 4, Section 4.12 and Article 20, Section 20.3.Z for a temporal modular classroom at Mildred Osborne Elementary School, an existing primary educational facility. Pursuant to Table 13-1, primary educational facilities are conditional uses within the site’s S-RS Single-Family Residential District. 

The temporal modular classroom would measure 24 ft in width, 64 ft in depth, and 1,536 sq ft in area. The modular classroom shall not affect existing parking or circulation as it will not be placed where it obstructs traffic. The existing school will remain. 


Eligible for 2year TCO once completed.

Phase One encompasses work required to make the facilities ready for a new congregation. This includes an overhaul of the mechanical systems serving the Educational Building and Gym/Fellowship Hall including both modified and entirely new systems. In addition to accounting for the power needs of new mechanical systems, the electrical scope includes swapping out existing light fixtures with new fixtures in the same location and providing new lighting and outlets to support services hosted in the Fellowship Hall. Plumbing upgrades include the relocation of an existing laundry room and the addition of two small restrooms in an area prior utilized as storage. Phase One also aims to address the roofing scope campus-wide to ensure watertight buildings before future improvements take place. Beyond system and envelope upgrades, interior finishes in these areas of the building will also be upgraded to avoid any future work requirements to the spaces in later phases of construction.

The scope of work consists of structurally renovating an existing convenience store building, renovating the pump island fascias, and installing new signage. The existing building will be about 50% partially demolished by removing the existing roof framing and some interior non-load-bearing walls. An addition to increase the Convenience Store sales area will be constructed on the left side of the building and a new corrugated metal roof will be constructed. The existing CMU walls will remain in place. A knee wall will be built to raise the ceiling height from 9 feet to 14 feet. The old shed building on the left side will be removed to allow for the addition to the existing Convenience Store. New landscaping and sidewalk repair is proposed. Existing fuel pumps will remain. No new pumps will be installed. The owner is only painting the columns and repairing the canopy ceilings and replacing the fascia with new metal panels and new signage.

The proposed project involved the renovation of the 2nd and 3rd floors of two connected buildings collectively known as "The Pythian." This scope of work includes the following:
•	Interior Demolition: complete interior demolition of all elements on the 2nd and 3rd floors, including the removal of walls, ceilings, fixtures, and mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) systems. Demolition of existing elevator and stairs at mezzanine. 
•	Structural Modification: Deleting and adding new structural steel to receive new ADA ramp where elevator and stair are currently located. 
•	Conversion to Residential Units: Conversion of the 2nd and 3rd floors into residential units, with services and chases aligned vertically with the residential units on the upper floors. New Gym and Social Lounge Spaces.
•	Lightwell Extension: Extension of the existing lightwell to the 2nd and 3rd floors to provide natural light to the rear residential units. Partial Demo of existing concrete slab and roof system for the installation of a new roof and pedestal paver system at the lightwell extension. Steel beams modifications to accept new roof.
•	Exclusions: The ground floor market, lobby, bike room, floors 4-9, and rooftop are excluded from the renovation scope.

The project consists of installing a rainwater harvesting system at the New Orleans Ernest N. Morial Convention Center to collect, filter, store, and reuse rainwater from the roof for non-potable applications. The system includes new piping, a pump room with a booster pump, large storage above ground cistern (850,000 gal), and a bridge spanning from MCCNO to the current Lot J above Henderson street. It is designed to reduce stormwater runoff, promote sustainability, and support the facility’s resilience goals

The project consists of a new detached 1-story building to house a constellation program on an existing school campus. It will house students with low incidence disabilities for grades Pre-K 4 through 4th grade. the building will have 10 new classrooms along with supporting infrastructure of restrooms, sensory rooms, therapy rooms, and administration area. The building will have modular building components built off-site and then cladding of brick and fiber cement panels, final roofing, and foundation built on site. Also, inclusive of this work will be a new car parking lot, a bus facility parking lot (ALTERNATES 1 & 2) , an artificial turf soccer field (ALTERNATE 5) and associated landscaping and fencing. 

PHASED RELEASE FOR FOUNDATION AND UNDERGROUND MEP IS RELEASED AS OF 6-9-25 PER CBO

The Iris Motel project proposes exterior improvements and a small accessory building addition to enhance the guest experience and expand the property’s hospitality offerings. The work focuses on transforming a portion of the existing pool deck and parking lot into an inviting outdoor gathering space featuring a new bar, kitchen, and landscaped patio.

The project includes the renovation of approximately 1,000 square feet of existing site area, including extension of the existing outdoor pool deck and a portion of the existing parking lot. The design scope provides for an approximately 500-square-foot accessory building containing a bar and kitchen, a partially covered outdoor patio, and new restrooms. Site upgrades include selective demolition, PERMEABLE PAVING, landscape planting, AND NEW SITE lighting. Seven existing parking spaces will be retained to satisfy minimum parking requirements per the New Orleans Comprehensive Zoning Ordinance (CZO).

*FINAL INSPECTION* New Accessory Building needs both a FEMA EC and a FEMA Dry Floodproofing Certificate certified by registered professional with floodproofing up to 0.8 NAVD
